disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms triggered by cold exposure this evening; both hands' digits went white, then blue, then red/throbbing (triphasic colour change) on getting cold
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me exactly what happened and what set it off?
What were you doing when your fingers changed colour?
Did anything trigger it — cold, for example?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started when I got cold tonight — the house is freezing. My fingers went completely white and numb, then a horrible blue-purple, and now they're going red and throbbing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known long-standing autoimmune condition with a positive ANA, not yet meeting criteria for a named connective-tissue disease; recurrent milder cold-triggered digital colour change in the past
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any medical conditions or ongoing health problems?
Has a doctor ever told you why your fingers do this?
Have you had any blood tests or a specialist for this before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've got some autoimmune thing my specialist hasn't fully pinned down — my ANA blood test was positive. My fingers have gone pale and numb in the cold before, but never this badly.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Screening for critical/irreversible digital ischaemia: currently NO digit is staying persistently white/cold/numb and none is blackening — the digits are reperfusing (colour returning, warming)
trigger_question_examples: Is any finger staying completely white, cold and numb and NOT getting its colour back?
Are any of your fingertips turning black or dark and staying that way?
Are your fingers warming up and the colour returning, or is one stuck white?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — they're actually going red and warming up now, the colour is coming back. None of them are staying white or turning black, they just really hurt as they warm.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I'm not sure, they just hurt a lot.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Widespread inflammatory joint aches and stiffness (wrists and knees feel puffy) with crushing systemic fatigue accompanying the episode
trigger_question_examples: Are you having any other symptoms — joint pain, stiffness, tiredness?
How are your joints and your energy levels?
Besides your hands, is anything else bothering you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My wrists and knees ache and feel a bit puffy and stiff, and I'm just crushingly tired — I can't get out of bed.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone and is currently unable to use hands to prepare food or open medication packaging — functional and social safety risk during the flare
trigger_question_examples: Is there anyone with you or who can check on you tonight?
Are you managing okay at home — food, medications, keeping warm?
You mentioned living alone — how are you coping with your hands like this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own and there's no one nearby. With my hands like this I can't cook or even open my tablets, and I'm stuck in bed.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No history of prior finger ulcers, skin breakdown or gangrene; no current open sores on the digits
trigger_question_examples: Have you ever had ulcers, sores or skin breakdown on your fingertips?
Is there any broken skin, blister or sore on your fingers right now?
Have any of your fingertips ever gone black or developed a wound?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I've never had sores or anything black on my fingers, and there's no broken skin now — it's just the colour and the pain.
